name: Kinetic Text Split

params.name: intensity
params.describe: reveal energy — sets stagger amount and the from-state

params.name: start
params.describe: scroll % where the first word begins (baked default; only override to retune)

params.name: end
params.describe: scroll % where the last word finishes (baked default)

license: MIT (@bsmnt/scrollytelling © 2023 basement.studio — LICENSE file verified) + GSAP under GreenSock's standard license, which became free for all commercial use in Apr 2025 after Webflow's acquisition (ScrollTrigger/SplitText now included free). Both deps confirmed free to ship.
notes: Per-word kinetic headline reveal on scroll via @bsmnt/scrollytelling. Wraps a SINGLE headline (wraps:headline) — lowest composition risk. Fits cinematic/immersive/editorial compositions but only when the run motion is kinetic/aggressive. Ships its own prefers-reduced-motion static fallback and default timing; the agent supplies only the headline text. Verified live by driving ScrollTrigger by hand: the four words stagger in on tweens at 0/14/28/41% and all resolve by 63% of the trigger range, from-state (opacity 0, yPercent 120, rotate 6deg) confirmed applied. Scroll range is h1 height + 180px, so the whole reveal lands in roughly 160-260px of scroll - deliberately snappy. Above-the-fold hero placement degrades safely: both trigger bounds compute negative, progress pins to 1 at scroll 0 and the headline renders fully visible and static rather than invisible.
